_meta.purpose: G40 (drug_comorbidity_safety) reference. ESTABLISHED FDA-label contraindication / warning CLASSES keyed by INN. NOT LLM clinical judgment — these are label-documented serious-risk classes (FDA prescribing information + NCI CTCAE 5.0). G40 uses this ONLY to decide *which patient comorbidity a given drug must be reconciled against*; the actual clinical call (whether to use the drug) is made by the LLM/prompt and recorded in claim.comorbidity_safety. The gate is mechanical: it checks the loop was closed.
_meta._note: Style mirrors knowledge/serious_risks_per_drug.json. Each contraindication-class carries `match_comorbidities`: substrings (lower-cased, ASCII + CJK) that, if present in a patient profile comorbidity string, mean this drug's labelled risk-class is RELEVANT to that patient and must be reconciled. memory:feedback_default_prompt_over_script — this is curated reference DATA (established FDA facts), not a keyword classifier doing clinical reasoning.
_meta.extension_protocol: To add a drug: include INN + class + contraindication_classes[] where each entry is {class, severity, match_comorbidities[], label_basis}. Brand names map to INN upstream (integrators/rxnorm.py) — do not duplicate by brand. Only add a contraindication-class that is an FDA boxed warning, contraindication, or major Warnings-&-Precautions item — not a routine low-grade AE.
_meta.match_semantics: match_comorbidities are matched case-insensitively as substrings against each comorbidity token in <patient_dir>/profile.json `comorbidities` (and any nested comorbidity strings). A hit means: this drug's labelled risk-class collides with a real patient comorbidity → claim.comorbidity_safety MUST close the loop (addressed==true AND the comorbidity considered).

contraindication_classes.label_basis: FDA label: CHF / arterial thromboembolic events; vasculotoxic agent — cardiac history must be reconciled.
FDA label: increased arterial thromboembolic events, esp. with prior arterial-thrombotic history.
FDA label: Grade 3-4 hypertension; pre-existing hypertension must be controlled.
FDA label: proteinuria / nephrotic syndrome; renal monitoring required.
FDA label: GI perforation, fistula, severe bleeding; recent surgery / GI history relevant.
FDA label: drug-induced hepatotoxicity (Grade 3-4 ALT/AST elevations); baseline hepatic impairment relevant.
FDA label: ILD / pneumonitis; pre-existing pulmonary disease relevant.
FDA label: QTc prolongation; baseline/concurrent QT risk must be reconciled.
FDA label: hepatotoxicity; baseline hepatic impairment relevant.
FDA label: ILD / pneumonitis.
FDA BOXED WARNING: severe and fatal hepatotoxicity.
FDA label: hypertension (incl. hypertensive crisis); pre-existing hypertension relevant.
FDA label: cardiac ischemia and infarction; cardiac history relevant.
FDA label: hypertension (incl. hypertensive crisis); pre-existing hypertension relevant.
FDA label: anti-angiogenic cardiovascular risk; cardiac history relevant.
FDA label: proteinuria; renal monitoring required.
FDA label: hypomagnesemia and accompanying electrolyte abnormalities; baseline electrolyte derangement relevant.
FDA label: severe acneiform / dermatologic toxicity; pre-existing severe dermatologic disease relevant.
FDA BOXED WARNING: infusion reactions incl. fatal cardiopulmonary arrest; CAD/CHF history relevant.
FDA label: electrolyte depletion (hypomagnesemia, hypocalcemia, hypokalemia).
FDA BOXED WARNING: dermatologic toxicity; pre-existing severe dermatologic disease relevant.
FDA BOXED WARNING: cardiomyopathy / LVEF decline / CHF; cardiac history must be reconciled.
